metadata.title: Compare Self-Check vs. Independent Verification on an Agent Output with Claude

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: This is Musinique. What stuck with me is that self-check rated all five claims verified yet missed the swapped citation on claim three, because the agent checks against the same representations it used to generate. I want to feel that gap myself. Help me: (1) generate a five-claim summary with one source per claim; (2) run the self-check pass and record its verdicts; (3) give me a checklist for opening each cited source independently so I can see exactly what only the human pass catches.

props.command: What stuck with me is that self-check rated all five claims verified yet missed the swapped citation on claim three, because the agent checks against the same representations it used to generate. I want to feel that gap myself. Help me: (1) generate a five-claim summary with one source per claim; (2) run the self-check pass and record its verdicts; (3) give me a checklist for opening each cited source independently so I can see exactly what only the human pass catches.
props.segment: Compare Self-Check vs. Independent Verification on

beats.narration_text: This is Musinique. Musinique. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

props.command: Compare Self-Check vs. Independent Verification on an Agent Output with Claude
props.segment: Compare Self-Check vs. Independent Verification on an Agent Output with Claude

beats.role_note: the problem, context, stakes -- BEFORE the CLI opens
beats.narration_text: Ask Claude to check its own answer: it finds two of six errors. Open the cited papers: you find five. Self-check improves at the margins but cannot catch the errors it made in the first place. The comparison makes the gap visible before you rely on self-check as your verification strategy.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- title card: SELF-CHECK FINDS 2 OF 6 ERRORS. Opening the cited papers finds 5.

beats.narration_text: The indie musician who automates their Spotify stats check, their distributor ping, their playlist pitch — that musician gets time back that they would otherwise spend refreshing a dashboard. An agent is not a luxury for the label's data team; it is a tool you can run free, on your own machine, today. The same frontmatter that tells Claude when to fire a code-review agent tells it when to check your streaming anomalies. You just haven't pointed it yet.

artifactLines: The same tools the platforms use — pointed back at you.
Roll your own. Own the deck.

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude to produce a five-claim research summary with one citation per claim, then self-check each claim against its cited source.

props.command: claude "Produce a five-claim summary of this research topic with one source citation per claim. Then, in a second pass, self-check each claim: is it supported by the source you cited? Rate each as verified / uncertain / unsupported."

beats.narration_text: The error comparison table builder outputs each claim with the self-check result and the human check result side by side -- showing which errors each method caught.

props.code:
RESULTS = [
 {"claim":1,"self":"verified",  "human":"verified",  "self_caught":"Y","human_caught":"Y"},
 {"claim":2,"self":"verified",  "human":"verified",  "self_caught":"Y","human_caught":"Y"},
 {"claim":3,"self":"verified",  "human":"UNSUPPORTED","self_caught":"N","human_caught":"Y"},
 {"claim":4,"self":"uncertain", "human":"verified",  "self_caught":"Y","human_caught":"Y"},
 {"claim":5,"self":"verified",  "human":"UNSUPPORTED","self_caught":"N","human_caught":"Y"},
]
hdr = "CLAIM  SELF-CHECK    HUMAN-CHECK   SELF-CAUGHT  HUMAN-CAUGHT"
print(hdr); print("-"*len(hdr))
for r in RESULTS:
    print(f"{r['claim']:<7}{r['self']:<14}{r['human']:<14}"
          f"{r['self_caught']:<13}{r['human_caught']}")
props.title: verify_compare.py

beats.narration_text: Self-check rates all five claims verified. The table shows no errors caught by self-check -- every claim passes the agent's own review.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- screen-recording: terminal showing five-claim table with all rows showing self-check=verified -- all SELF-CAUGHT=Y, no errors detected by agent self-check

beats.narration_text: Replace claim three's citation with a paper that does not support it. Re-run the self-check -- does it catch the wrong citation?

props.command: claude "Replace claim 3's citation with a paper that does NOT support it. Re-run the self-check -- does it catch the wrong citation?"

beats.narration_text: Self-check rates claim three verified with the wrong citation. Human check opens the paper and flags it UNSUPPORTED. The self-check missed the error it introduced.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- screen-recording: revised table showing claim 3 row with self-check=verified, human-check=UNSUPPORTED in red, SELF-CAUGHT=N, HUMAN-CAUGHT=Y -- self-check failed to catch its own wrong citation

beats.narration_text: Self-check improves output at the margins but cannot catch systematic errors -- the agent checks its claims against the same representations it used to generate them. Independent verification is what makes 'verified' mean something.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- recap card: SELF-CHECK CANNOT CATCH WHAT IT MADE. Independent verification is what 'verified' means.

beats.narration_text: For any consequential research output, open the cited sources yourself and verify each claim. Self-check is a first pass -- independent verification is what you require before accepting the output.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- next-steps card: Open the cited sources. Verify each claim independently. Self-check is a first pass, not verification.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. Self-check improves output at the margins but cannot catch systematic errors -- the agent checks its claims against the same representations it used to generate them. Independent verification is what makes 'verified' mean something. For any consequential research output, open the cited sources yourself and verify each claim. Self-check is a first pass -- independent verification is what you require before accepting the output.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Compare Self-Check vs. Independent Verification on an Agent Output wit] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
